data.title: Why Is System1 Stock Sliding Monday?
data.description:
Nasdaq futures are up 0.77% while S&P 500 futures have gained 0.77%.

Technical Reversal Follows Massive Gains

The stock skyrocketed 142.34% on Friday to close at $3.32. This move occurred without a specific news catalyst on that day. The premarket reversal indicates traders are likely taking profits after the volatile spike.

Recent Earnings Performance

On March 11, the company reported a fourth-quarter loss of $1.65 per share. This result beat the analyst loss consensus estimate of $2.02 by 18.32%.

However, revenue told a different story. Quarterly sales hit $51.940 million, which missed the analyst consensus estimate of $53.036 million by 2.07%, according to Benzinga Pro.

Sales Decline and Business Focus

The reported revenue represents a 31.29% decrease from the previous year.

System1 operates an AI-powered responsive acquisition marketing platform. It generates most of its revenue from its Marketing segment. The company focuses on consumer verticals like shopping, travel, and search.

Technical Analysis

SST is trading 22.9% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), but 24.1% below its 100-day SMA, a split that often signals a short-term rebound inside a still-damaged intermediate trend.

Shares are down 12.93% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs within the $1.35 to $15.00 range.

The RSI is at 62.29, which sits in neutral territory MACD is at -0.3794 versus a signal line at -0.4683.

  • Key Resistance: $3.00
  • Key Support: $2.50

SST Stock Price Activity: System1 shares were down 14.46% at $2.84 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by PeachShutterStock via Shutterstock

data.title: US-Iran War Updates March 30: Trump Threatens Iran Over Hormuz, Spain Blocks US From Using Bases, Iranian Leadership Divide Emerges (UPDATED)
data.description:
Here are the latest developments in the U.S.–Israel–Iran war on Monday at 3:43 AM ET, as the conflict enters its fifth week since it began on February 28.

Islamabad Talks End, Leaders Push For Ceasefire

On Monday, foreign ministers from Saudi Arabia, Türkiye, and Egypt concluded talks in Islamabad, where Pakistan emphasized dialogue as the only path forward. The four nations jointly called for an immediate ceasefire, stressed Muslim unity, and supported structured U.S.–Iran negotiations. Earlier, Pakistan Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar offered to host "meaningful" talks in the country to help end the war amid a push for de-escalation.

Qatar Condemns Iran Strikes On Kuwait

Qatar strongly condemned Iran's strikes on key infrastructure in Kuwait, including a camp, power station, and desalination plant, calling the attacks unjustified and expressing full solidarity with Kuwait. Kuwaiti authorities said the strike killed an Indian worker and damaged facilities at the site, reported Al Jazeera.

Iran Power Rift Deepens Amid Crisis

Iran Executes Two Over MEK-Linked Attacks

Iran executed two individuals accused of links to the opposition group Mojahedin-e-Khalq (MEK), with authorities alleging they carried out attacks on security forces amid connections to the U.S. and Israel, reported Al Jazeera.

War Displaces 20% of Lebanon's Population — UNICEF

The war has displaced 20% of Lebanon's population in three weeks, with around 19,000 children forced to flee daily, UNICEF told CNN. It also stated that reaching affected children, especially in southern areas, remains a major challenge.

Photo courtesy: Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Wayfair Stock Monday?
data.description:
Nasdaq futures are up 0.69% while S&P 500 futures have gained 0.73%.

Massive Insider Share Offload

Recent filings show heavy selling by top brass. CEO Niraj Shah sold 109,389 shares at a $77.19 weighted average. He also offloaded 10,611 additional shares. These transactions left him with 49,137 direct shares.

Director Joins Selling Spree

Director Steven Conine mirrored these moves. Conine sold 109,606 shares at a $77.19 weighted average price. Further transactions involved 10,394 shares at prices up to $79.34.

High Short Interest Persistence

Benzinga data shows short interest in Wayfair recently decreased from 19.03 million to 18.73 million shares. Despite this, 23.48% of the float remains short. Based on average volume, shorts need 5.52 days to cover positions.

Technical Analysis

Wayfair is trading 6.9% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 25.9% below its 100-day SMA, keeping the intermediate trend pointed down even as the stock tries to stabilize.

Shares are up 123.23% over the past 12 months, but the current price is positioned closer to the middle of its 52-week range than the January highs.

The RSI is at 39.19, which sits in neutral territory. Meanwhile, MACD is at -2.9426 versus a signal line at -3.4561.

  • Key Resistance: $81.50
  • Key Support: $70.50

W Stock Price Activity: Wayfair shares were down 1.10% at $70.71 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Tada Images via Shutterstock

data.title: Inside The Viral 2026 Resurgence Of Ripple's 'Hypothetical' Listing Fee Story
data.description:
The Hypothetical That Wasn’t So Hypothetical

A month later, he followed up with a disclaimer that he was about to tell a “completely made up, hypothetical story” where any resemblance to real exchanges was “entirely coincidental.”

In his hypothetical, an unnamed exchange refuses to list XRP even though doing so would clearly benefit their business. 

Instead, they approach Ripple, demanding millions of dollars as a listing fee. Ripple refuses, and months go by at a deadlock. 

The exchange even privately admits that they would have listed XRP long ago if Ripple simply didn’t exist as a company.

Eventually, a deal gets struck. Ripple pays. XRP gets listed. Almost immediately, XRP trading accounts for 20% of the exchange’s revenue, proving the token was always going to be a money-maker.

Why Ripple Paid

Schwartz was not celebrating this outcome. His point was that Ripple essentially had to pay money just to stop their own existence from being used as an excuse to block XRP from the market. 

He framed it as damage control, writing “we paid money to not let our existence hurt the ecosystem.”

The deeper frustration centered on the SEC lawsuit. 

Schwartz noted that a litigation adversary was cherry-picking these very facts to argue that Ripple was paying exchanges to artificially pump XRP adoption and liquidity, a framing he found deeply unfair and misleading.

The Viral Misinterpretation

Photo: Grey82 / Shutterstock.com

data.title: Palantir Lands Expanded Stellantis Deal To Scale AI Across Operations
data.description:
Expanded Platform Deployment

Stellantis will expand its use of Palantir Foundry and begin deploying the Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) across select functions and regions. Foundry centralizes and operationalizes data, while AIP integrates AI into workflows with governance controls.

Operational Impact And Integration

The combined platforms aim to unify fragmented datasets, improve transparency, and enable faster decision-making within industrial operations.

AIP will build on Stellantis’ existing data structure in Foundry, linking generative AI with internal data and business processes. The approach is intended to strengthen governance, improve traceability, and support the scaled deployment of AI use cases.

The initiative also supports Stellantis’ Data4All strategy by expanding secure data access across teams.

François Bohuon, General Manager of Palantir France and EMEA Executive, and Grégoire Omont, Europe Operations Lead, stated: “By combining Foundry and AIP, we are helping Stellantis embed secure, governed AI at the heart of its operations — turning data into a decisive advantage across every function and geography.”

PLTR 5.5% Below 20-Day SMA

Currently, the stock is trading 5.5% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 12.2% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend in the short- to medium-term. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 69.50%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 42.24, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at 0.7638, below its signal line at 1.3955, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $161.50
  • Key Support: $126.50

PLTR May 4, 2026 Earnings

Palantir Technologies is slated to provide its next financial update on May 4, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: 26 cents (Up from 13 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.54 billion (Up from $883.86 million)
  • Valuation: P/E of 227.1x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $173.11. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Rosenblatt: Buy (Maintains Target to $200.00) (Mar. 25)
  • UBS: Buy (Raises Target to $200.00) (Mar. 18)
  • Wedbush: Outperform (Maintains Target to $230.00) (Mar. 16)

Benzinga Edge: Growth 98.56, Value 1.45

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Palantir Technologies, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Weak (Score: 1.45) — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Growth: Strong (Score: 98.56) — High growth potential indicated by strong metrics.
  • Momentum: Neutral (Score: 68.79) — Mixed signals in recent trading activity.

The Verdict: Palantir Benzinga Edge signal reveals a growth-heavy profile, suggesting strong market potential despite current value concerns.

PLTR ETF Weights: AIPI 8.93%, IGV 8.32%

Significance: Because PLTR carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Stock Price Activity: PLTR shares were up 0.52% at $143.80, and STLA was up 1.79% at $6.83 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.
